description: Promote discovery module from migrate/discovery.rs to top-level libaipm::discovery with its own error type
steps: Create crates/libaipm/src/discovery.rs with contents of crates/libaipm/src/migrate/discovery.rs
Define a self-contained discovery::Error enum with WalkFailed(String) variant, replacing use of migrate::Error
Update all functions in the new module to return discovery::Error instead of migrate::Error
Add pub mod discovery; to crates/libaipm/src/lib.rs
Update crates/libaipm/src/migrate/mod.rs to replace pub mod discovery; with pub use crate::discovery; (or update internal references)
Update crates/libaipm/src/migrate/dry_run.rs import from use super::discovery::DiscoveredSource to use crate::discovery::DiscoveredSource
Update the discover_source_dirs() call in migrate/mod.rs to use crate::discovery::discover_source_dirs and map discovery::Error to migrate::Error
Replace migrate::Error::DiscoveryFailed with a From<discovery::Error> impl on migrate::Error
Delete the old crates/libaipm/src/migrate/discovery.rs file
Run cargo build --workspace to verify compilation
Run cargo test --workspace to verify all existing discovery tests pass
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings to verify no lint issues
Run cargo fmt --check to verify formatting

description: Add DiscoveryFailed error variant to lint::Error with From<discovery::Error> impl
steps: Open crates/libaipm/src/lint/mod.rs and locate the Error enum
Add a new variant: DiscoveryFailed(String) with #[error("discovery failed: {0}")] annotation
Add impl From<crate::discovery::Error> for Error that converts discovery::Error to DiscoveryFailed
Run cargo build --workspace to verify compilation
Add a unit test in lint/mod.rs tests verifying the error Display output
Run cargo test --workspace to verify tests pass

description: Modify lint() pipeline to use recursive discovery for .claude/.github source directories
steps: Open crates/libaipm/src/lint/mod.rs and locate the lint() function
Replace the flat fs.exists() source detection block (lines 47-63) with a two-phase approach
Phase 1: Determine source patterns based on opts.source -- vec![] for .ai, vec![src] for explicit .claude/.github, vec!['.claude', '.github'] for auto-discover
Call crate::discovery::discover_source_dirs(&opts.dir, &source_patterns, opts.max_depth) when patterns are non-empty
Iterate over each DiscoveredSource, get rules via rules::for_source(&src.source_type), run rule.check(&src.source_dir, fs)
Apply config suppression, severity overrides, and ignore path filtering to diagnostics from each discovered source
Phase 2: Determine if .ai/ marketplace should be checked -- true for --source .ai or auto-discover when fs.exists(.ai/)
If checking marketplace, run rules::for_marketplace() against opts.dir.join('.ai') with same config/ignore logic as before
Deduplicate sources_scanned entries so multiple .claude/ dirs produce a single '.claude' entry
Ensure diagnostics are still sorted by file_path for consistent output
Remove the unused source_dir variable assignment (let source_dir = opts.dir.join(source_type.trim_start_matches('.'));)
Run cargo build --workspace to verify compilation
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings
Run cargo fmt --check

description: Update cmd_lint() CLI validation to relax --source existence check for .claude/.github
steps: Open crates/aipm/src/main.rs and locate the cmd_lint() function
Keep the SUPPORTED_SOURCES validation (unsupported source type check)
Change the source directory existence check: only validate root-level existence for --source .ai
For --source .claude and --source .github, remove the root-level existence check -- let discovery handle it
Run cargo build --workspace to verify compilation
Run cargo test --workspace to verify existing E2E tests still pass
Verify that aipm lint --source .ai in a dir without .ai/ still errors correctly

description: Unit tests for recursive discovery integration in lint pipeline
steps: Add test lint_discovers_nested_claude_dirs: create tempdir with packages/auth/.claude/skills/ and .ai/, run lint(), verify misplaced-features diagnostic fires for nested dir
Add test lint_discovers_nested_github_dirs: same pattern for .github/ with nested .github/skills/
Add test lint_source_filter_claude_only: create both nested .claude/ and .github/ dirs, lint with source=Some('.claude'), verify only .claude diagnostics
Add test lint_source_filter_ai_skips_discovery: lint with source=Some('.ai'), verify no discovery is run for .claude/.github
Add test lint_max_depth_limits_discovery: create root .claude/ and nested packages/auth/.claude/, lint with max_depth=Some(1), verify only root is found
Add test lint_no_sources_found_succeeds: lint an empty tempdir, verify empty outcome with no errors
Add test lint_deduplicates_sources_scanned: create multiple .claude/ dirs, verify sources_scanned contains single '.claude' entry
Run cargo test --workspace to verify all tests pass

Run cargo +nightly llvm-cov clean --workspace && cargo +nightly llvm-cov --no-report --workspace --branch && cargo +nightly llvm-cov --no-report --doc && cargo +nightly llvm-cov report --doctests --branch --ignore-filename-regex '(tests/|research/|specs/|wizard_tty\.rs)' to verify >= 89% branch coverage

description: E2E tests for monorepo lint scenarios
steps: Open crates/aipm/tests/lint_e2e.rs

Add test lint_monorepo_finds_nested_misplaced_features: create tempdir monorepo with .ai/ at root and packages/auth/.claude/skills/, run aipm lint, verify stdout contains source/misplaced-features with nested path

Add test lint_source_claude_no_root_dir_succeeds_with_nested: create tempdir with .ai/ at root and packages/auth/.claude/skills/ but NO root .claude/, run aipm lint --source .claude, verify it succeeds and finds the nested diagnostic
Add test lint_max_depth_cli_flag: create tempdir with root .claude/skills/ and nested packages/auth/.claude/skills/ plus .ai/, run aipm lint --max-depth 1, verify only root diagnostic found
Run cargo test --workspace to verify all E2E tests pass

Run cargo +nightly llvm-cov clean --workspace && cargo +nightly llvm-cov --no-report --workspace --branch && cargo +nightly llvm-cov --no-report --doc && cargo +nightly llvm-cov report --doctests --branch --ignore-filename-regex '(tests/|research/|specs/|wizard_tty\.rs)' to verify >= 89% branch coverage

description: Verify all existing tests pass and no regressions introduced
steps: Run cargo build --workspace to verify full compilation
Run cargo test --workspace to verify all tests pass (existing + new)
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings to verify no clippy warnings
Run cargo fmt --check to verify formatting

Run cargo +nightly llvm-cov clean --workspace && cargo +nightly llvm-cov --no-report --workspace --branch && cargo +nightly llvm-cov --no-report --doc && cargo +nightly llvm-cov report --doctests --branch --ignore-filename-regex '(tests/|research/|specs/|wizard_tty\.rs)' to verify >= 89% branch coverage
Verify existing lint_e2e.rs tests still pass (root-level directories are still discovered)
Verify existing discovery.rs tests pass in their new location
Verify existing misplaced_features.rs unit tests pass unchanged
